prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(discover_component source_component_instance_a)
(discover_component target_component_instance_a)
(discover_component migration_project_alpha)
(discover_component migration_project_beta)
(assign_component_to_environment source_component_instance_a source_deployment_environment)
(assign_component_to_environment target_component_instance_a target_deployment_environment)
(attach_interface_specification source_component_instance_a interface_specification_baseline)
(confirm_interface_specification source_component_instance_a interface_specification_baseline)
(attach_interface_adapter source_component_instance_a interface_adapter_connector)
(prepare_source_interface source_component_instance_a source_interface_endpoint interface_specification_baseline)
(mark_source_component_ready source_component_instance_a source_interface_endpoint interface_adapter_connector)
(detach_interface_adapter source_component_instance_a interface_adapter_connector)
(detach_interface_specification source_component_instance_a interface_specification_baseline)
(attach_interface_specification target_component_instance_a interface_specification_baseline)
(confirm_interface_specification target_component_instance_a interface_specification_baseline)
(attach_interface_adapter target_component_instance_a interface_adapter_connector)
(prepare_target_interface target_component_instance_a target_interface_endpoint interface_specification_baseline)
(mark_target_component_ready target_component_instance_a target_interface_endpoint interface_adapter_connector)
(detach_interface_adapter target_component_instance_a interface_adapter_connector)
(detach_interface_specification target_component_instance_a interface_specification_baseline)
(assemble_migration_bundle source_component_instance_a target_component_instance_a source_interface_endpoint target_interface_endpoint migration_bundle_artifact)
(attach_interface_specification source_component_instance_a interface_specification_baseline)
(finalize_migration_bundle migration_bundle_artifact source_component_instance_a interface_specification_baseline)
(activate_source_component source_component_instance_a migration_bundle_artifact)
(activate_target_component target_component_instance_a migration_bundle_artifact)
(start_component_cutover source_component_instance_a rollback_contingency_plan interface_specification_baseline)
(finalize_source_component_migration source_component_instance_a source_deployment_environment rollback_contingency_plan)
(detach_interface_specification source_component_instance_a interface_specification_baseline)
(attach_interface_specification target_component_instance_a interface_specification_baseline)
(start_component_cutover target_component_instance_a rollback_contingency_plan interface_specification_baseline)
(finalize_target_component_migration target_component_instance_a target_deployment_environment rollback_contingency_plan)
(detach_interface_specification target_component_instance_a interface_specification_baseline)
(assign_component_to_environment migration_project_alpha validation_deployment_environment)
(attach_interface_specification migration_project_alpha interface_specification_baseline)
(confirm_interface_specification migration_project_alpha interface_specification_baseline)
(link_schema_to_bundle migration_project_alpha source_data_schema migration_bundle_artifact)
(validate_schema_for_project migration_project_alpha source_data_schema migration_bundle_artifact interface_specification_baseline)
(attach_performance_profile migration_project_alpha performance_sla_profile)
(attach_interface_adapter migration_project_alpha interface_adapter_connector)
(initiate_project_review_base migration_project_alpha performance_sla_profile interface_adapter_connector source_data_schema migration_bundle_artifact)
(detach_interface_adapter migration_project_alpha interface_adapter_connector)
(attach_approval_token migration_project_alpha approval_signoff_token)
(request_release_signoff_base migration_project_alpha approval_signoff_token source_data_schema migration_bundle_artifact)
(detach_performance_profile migration_project_alpha performance_sla_profile)
(detach_approval_token migration_project_alpha approval_signoff_token)
(authorize_release migration_project_alpha)
(start_component_cutover migration_project_alpha rollback_contingency_plan interface_specification_baseline)
(finalize_project_migration migration_project_alpha validation_deployment_environment rollback_contingency_plan)
(detach_interface_specification migration_project_alpha interface_specification_baseline)
(assign_component_to_environment migration_project_beta validation_deployment_environment)
(attach_interface_specification migration_project_beta interface_specification_baseline)
(confirm_interface_specification migration_project_beta interface_specification_baseline)
(link_schema_to_bundle migration_project_beta target_data_schema migration_bundle_artifact)
(validate_schema_for_project migration_project_beta target_data_schema migration_bundle_artifact interface_specification_baseline)
(attach_performance_profile migration_project_beta performance_sla_profile)
(attach_interface_adapter migration_project_beta interface_adapter_connector)
(initiate_project_review_base migration_project_beta performance_sla_profile interface_adapter_connector target_data_schema migration_bundle_artifact)
(detach_interface_adapter migration_project_beta interface_adapter_connector)
(attach_approval_token migration_project_beta approval_signoff_token)
(request_release_signoff_base migration_project_beta approval_signoff_token target_data_schema migration_bundle_artifact)
(detach_performance_profile migration_project_beta performance_sla_profile)
(detach_approval_token migration_project_beta approval_signoff_token)
(authorize_release migration_project_beta)
(start_component_cutover migration_project_beta rollback_contingency_plan interface_specification_baseline)
(finalize_project_migration migration_project_beta validation_deployment_environment rollback_contingency_plan)
```

## Explanation of Generated Plan

The plan systematically migrates the components and projects by:
1. Discovering all relevant entities.
2. Assigning components to appropriate environments.
message.reasoning:
The problem is a phased component migration with two projects: alpha and beta, both involving the same source and target components, but different schemas and stakeholders. The goal is to migrate source_component_instance_a, target_component_instance_a, migration_project_alpha, and migration_project_beta.


## Examining migration details  
- The task involves migrating components across two projects, alpha and beta, both sharing the same source and target components.  
- Each project has unique schemas and stakeholders, affecting the migration process.  
- The components to migrate include source_component_instance_a, target_component_instance_a, migration_project_alpha, and migration_project_beta.

## Examining migration details  
- Migration involves a series of steps like discovering entities, assigning to environments, and verifying interfaces, but complexities arise from shared bundles and schemas.  
- The initial state includes available environments and resources, but no entities are discovered yet.  
- To achieve migration, all four entities—source_component_instance_a, target_component_instance_a, migration_project_alpha, and migration_project_beta—must reach the entity_migrated state.  

## Planning migration steps  
- Start by discovering all four components using the discover_component action, as it applies to both application_components and migration_projects.  
- Assign components to environments: source to source_deployment_environment, target to target_deployment_environment, and projects likely to validation_deployment_environment.  

## Addressing environment constraints  
- With only three environments for four components, consider sequential assignment, as assigning consumes environment availability until finalized.  
- Finalizing migration makes environments available again, suggesting a phased approach where components are migrated before projects or interleaved for efficiency.

## Addressing environment constraints  
- With four entities and only three environments, migration must occur sequentially, freeing up environments after finalizing one entity.  
- To proceed, assign and migrate one project and its shared components, then reassign environments for the next project.  

## Evaluating project migration  
- Projects require schema validation and release authorization to reach migration_ready, involving steps like schema_locked and bundle_assembled.  
- Components must be active and interfaces ready before bundle assembly, ensuring all entities are prepared for cutover.

## Evaluating interface preparation  
- After verifying interfaces, components need their interface specifications attached to prepare for migration, like preparing source interfaces.  
- Detaching specifications post-verification can disrupt preparation, as actions like prepare_source_interface require the specification to remain attached.

## Exploring adapter attachment  
- Attaching interface adapters is necessary before marking components ready, requiring prior interface verification and adapter availability.  
- Components, including projects, can attach adapters without needing specifications reattached, simplifying the process.

## Analyzing readiness paths  
- To make components active and ready, options include preparing interfaces and attaching adapters, then marking them ready, or reserving and releasing migration tools.  
- Assembling migration bundles varies based on component readiness, with different branches for fully ready, source-only committed, target-only committed, or both committed but not ready states.

## Managing adapter attachment  
- Adapters must be attached to source components first, then detached to attach to target components for marking readiness.  
- After marking source ready, detach the adapter from the source and reattach it to the target, ensuring both can be marked ready sequentially.

## Sequencing component preparation  
- Components like source_a and target_a require environment assignment before interface preparation, starting with discovery and validation.  
- After attaching and confirming specifications, prepare interfaces and mark components active and ready, then detach for reuse on the target.

## Handling specification lifecycle  
- Specifications must remain attached to source components for finalizing migration bundles, requiring reattachment after assembly if detached.  
- Detaching specifications post-preparation makes them available for target use, but reattachment is necessary before finalizing to meet requirements.

## Linking project schemas  
- Linking schemas to bundles requires a verified project and a locked, embedded schema, ensuring data consistency.  
- Validating schemas for projects involves checking verification, schema embedding, and attached specifications, leading to schema validation.

## Attaching project profiles  
- Attaching performance profiles to projects requires verification and profile availability, linking them for review processes.  
- Projects need adapters attached before initiating reviews, ensuring readiness for performance and schema evaluation.

## Initiating project reviews  
- Initiating project reviews requires validated schemas, performance profiles, and attached adapters, moving projects to under-review status.  
- Projects need approval tokens attached post-review initiation, ensuring they meet release signoff requirements.

## Managing release approvals  
- Requesting release signoffs requires under-review status, approval tokens, and embedded schemas, leading to approval.  
- Authorizing releases follows approval, marking projects migration-ready for final cutover steps.

## Managing project transitions  
- After alpha project reaches under-review, detach the adapter and token, making them available for reuse.  
- Detach performance profile from alpha post-approval, ensuring resources are free for subsequent projects like beta.  

## Preparing beta project  
- Assign beta to a validation environment after alpha cutover, ensuring readiness for interface preparation.  
- Attach specifications to beta, confirm verification, and link schemas to bundles for consistency.  

## Reusing resources for beta  
- Reattach available adapters and tokens to beta, initiating review processes as done for alpha.  
- Detach resources like performance profiles and tokens after beta approval, preparing for final steps.  

## Finalizing project cutovers  
- Authorize release for beta after approval, marking it migration-ready for cutover, similar to alpha.  
- Start cutover for beta with rollback plans and finalize migration, making the environment available again.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
